seo.defaultDescription: PracticalKit: browser-first utilities for PDF, images, data, text, developer workflows, and lightweight media conversion.
seo.homeDescription: PracticalKit — browser tools for PDF, images, data, and developer utilities. Local processing when possible; multilingual; free online utilities.

legalStrip.title: Transparency & policies (AdSense / publishers)
legalStrip.subtitle: Read Privacy, Terms, and Disclaimer — especially for cookies, ads, and third-party APIs.

adsPlacement.disclosure: Sponsored — shown only after on-page editorial content has loaded.

homePro.trust1Body: Most utilities run in your browser. Files and pasted text often never reach our servers—check each tool page for the exact data path.
homePro.trust2Body: Translate and FX tools name their API providers up front. We do not hide off-device traffic behind vague “online tool” wording.
homePro.trust3Body: About, Blog, Privacy, and per-tool guides explain scope, limits, and updates so pages stand on their own—not just a form in an empty shell.
homePro.categoriesTitle: Browse by what you need to do
homePro.categoriesLead: Professional tool sites group work by outcome. Start from a category, then open a focused utility with its own explanation block at the bottom of the page.
homePro.category_documents: Merge, split, compress, and export PDFs without installing desktop software when the browser can handle the job.
homePro.category_media: Resize, convert, and inspect images, audio, and video with realistic browser limits called out on each page.
homePro.category_dataDev: Format JSON/XML/YAML, diff configs, encode strings, and ship small developer helpers—all with local processing where possible.
homePro.category_daily: Timestamps, units, passwords, QR codes, colors, and quick conversions for everyday tasks.
homePro.category_math: Plot functions, run a scientific calculator, and convert units with readable on-page notes.
homePro.category_extras: Hashes, JWT decode, cron helpers, HTTP status lookup, and other sharp utilities for builders.
homePro.step1Title: Choose a category or search the grid
homePro.step1Body: Land on a tool that matches your task. Each listing includes a short blurb so you know what you are opening before you click.
homePro.step2Title: Read scope on the tool page
homePro.step2Body: Above the controls you get the workflow; below you get data handling, limits, and links to policies—written for humans and crawlers alike.
homePro.step3Body: Run the utility, download results, and keep originals. If something needs a network API, the page says so before you paste sensitive data.
homePro.faq1Q: Is this site only thin calculators?
homePro.faq1A: No. Every tool route includes a substantive footer guide (processing mode, limits, policy links). The homepage, About, and Blog add standalone editorial content—not just navigation chrome.
homePro.faq2Q: Why are ads below the content?
homePro.faq2A: We place ads only after the main article/tool text is present in the page, matching Google’s requirement not to show ads on screens without publisher content.
homePro.faq3Q: Can I use this for regulated or secret data?
homePro.faq3A: Treat each tool’s on-page notice as authoritative. Prefer local-only tools for secrets; avoid pasting regulated personal data into API-backed utilities.

toolEditorial.intro: You are using “{toolName}” on PracticalKit. The section below is editorial context—how data is handled, typical limits, and where to read policies—separate from the interactive controls above.

local.title: In-browser processing for this utility
local.p1: For “{toolName}”, the interactive logic runs in your web browser on this device. PracticalKit’s hosting delivers HTML, assets, and scripts; we do not run a companion backend that ingests your pasted text or uploaded files for this specific tool unless an on-page notice says otherwise.
local.p2: Practical limits still apply: available RAM, JavaScript execution time, codec or WASM support, and browser security rules (clipboard, storage, file handles). Very large inputs may be slow or fail; keep originals whenever the output matters for work, compliance, or archives.
local.p3: Optional ads or analytics (only after cookie consent where required) may load third-party scripts as described in Privacy—that is separate from the tool’s own computation path. If this explanation disagrees with what you observe in DevTools, contact us with the tool URL and we will correct the copy.

thirdParty.title: This tool uses a third-party API
thirdParty.p1: “{toolName}” sends the text or parameters you provide to an external provider named in the on-tool notice. PracticalKit does not silently route that payload through our own servers unless the page explicitly states a proxy or server step.
thirdParty.p2: Do not paste secrets, regulated personal data, or content you are not allowed to transmit. Providers can change quotas, models, or availability; results may vary by region. When you need strictly local handling, pick another utility on this site that is documented as browser-only.
thirdParty.p3: Cookies, advertising tags, and analytics are covered under Privacy and Terms. Read Disclaimer before relying on outputs for legal, medical, financial, or safety-critical decisions.

fileMedia.title: Files, PDFs, and media in the browser
fileMedia.p1: “{toolName}” reads files you select in the browser. Where the implementation allows, bytes are processed with Web APIs or WASM on your device without PracticalKit storing the file on our servers for the conversion itself.
fileMedia.p2: PDF, image, audio, and video workflows can be memory-heavy. Success depends on file size, resolution, encoding, and device RAM. If the browser aborts or freezes, try a smaller asset, a simpler format, or desktop software for very large productions.
fileMedia.p3: Always keep backups of originals. These pages are general-purpose utilities, not professional services. See Disclaimer; use Contact if privacy wording on this route is inaccurate.

home.welcomeLead: A calm, browser-first toolbox for PDFs, images, data, and everyday conversions. We prefer local processing so your files stay on your device whenever the tool allows it.
home.recentEmpty: Open any tool below — we keep your last picks on this device only (nothing is sent to our servers for this list).
home.recentHint: Stored in your browser. When you change language, these links open in your current locale.
home.qualityLead: Search and advertising quality systems may label a site low-value when most URLs look like thin utility shells with little surrounding explanation. PracticalKit publishes this homepage narrative, an expanded About page, and a Blog with articles that explain engineering choices, data flows, and update history—without requiring you to run a tool first.
home.qualityP2: Each utility page states scope clearly: what runs locally, what calls third parties, approximate browser limits, and what we do not collect on our origin. That transparency is the same standard we apply when preparing the site for monetization: substantive editorial pages first, then tools.
home.lead: Browser-first utilities for everyday work. Processing stays in your device when possible; see each tool for details.
home.privacyNote: Designed for Pages/CDN hosting. Prefer client-side processing to keep your files off servers.
home.editorialBody:
PracticalKit is maintained by a solo publisher who builds small, focused browser utilities for everyday work — PDFs, images, data formats, developer helpers, and light media tasks.

The goal is simple: fast pages, clear UX, and privacy-friendly defaults (client-side processing whenever the tool allows it). The site is updated as new utilities ship or existing ones improve; see the Blog for a short changelog and practical notes.
home.editorialUpdate: May update: refreshed UI language, faster transitions, new loading motion, and two new tools (Text diff + Base converter).

toolBlurbs.blog: Changelog and notes so you know what changed and why.
toolBlurbs.pdf: Merge, split, compress, and convert PDFs in your browser when possible.
toolBlurbs.image: Resize, compress, convert formats, and run quick image tweaks locally.
toolBlurbs.data: Format, validate, and convert JSON, XML, and YAML without leaving the tab.
toolBlurbs.text: Count words, change case, dedupe lines, sort, and batch-edit text.
toolBlurbs.qr: Create QR codes and read barcodes from images for quick sharing.
toolBlurbs.units: Convert length, weight, temperature, and other everyday units.
toolBlurbs.time: Translate Unix timestamps and compare time zones at a glance.
toolBlurbs.functionPlot: Plot expressions on axes to visualize math functions quickly.
toolBlurbs.calc: Scientific calculator with expressions and a simple history.
toolBlurbs.encoding: Encode and decode Base64, URL-safe strings, and hex dumps.
toolBlurbs.password: Generate strong passwords and estimate strength before you save them.
toolBlurbs.translate: Translate pasted text via a third-party API — check the on-page notice.
toolBlurbs.code: Minify, format, and convert small code snippets for sharing.
toolBlurbs.money: Look up FX rates with clear source and timestamp (API-backed).
toolBlurbs.markdown: Preview Markdown and copy clean HTML for posts or docs.
toolBlurbs.random: UUIDs, random strings, dice rolls, and IDs for tests or mocks.
toolBlurbs.jsonDiff: Compare two JSON trees and spot what actually changed.
toolBlurbs.hash: Compute SHA and other digests entirely in your browser.
toolBlurbs.jwt: Decode JWT header and payload — verify signatures with your own keys.
toolBlurbs.cron: Turn cron strings into human-readable schedules and next runs.
toolBlurbs.httpStatus: Look up status codes with short explanations for APIs and debugging.
toolBlurbs.apiSnippet: Turn endpoints into curl or fetch snippets you can paste into projects.
toolBlurbs.csvJson: Convert between CSV and JSON with delimiter and header options.
toolBlurbs.slug: Build URL-safe slugs and tidy query strings for clean links.
toolBlurbs.htmlEntities: Escape or unescape entities so markup stays safe or readable.
toolBlurbs.cssMin: Strip whitespace and comments from CSS for smaller payloads.
toolBlurbs.lorem: Generate placeholder paragraphs when layouts need filler text.
toolBlurbs.unicode: Inspect code points, normalize strings, and debug odd characters.
toolBlurbs.cidr: Split subnets, list host ranges, and sanity-check network masks.
toolBlurbs.color: Pick colors and convert HEX, RGB, and HSL for design handoff.
toolBlurbs.videoGif: Convert or compress video and GIF with clear format limits per browser.
toolBlurbs.audio: Transcode common audio formats where the browser allows decoding.
toolBlurbs.regex: Test patterns with live highlights before you ship them to prod.
toolBlurbs.textDiff: Compare two text blocks by lines or words and focus on exact edits.
toolBlurbs.baseConvert: Convert large integers between base2, base8, base10, base16, and base36.
toolBlurbs.rgbv3d: Decode RGBV3D payloads and preview the packed image data.

aboutPage.title: About PracticalKit
aboutPage.p1: PracticalKit is a browser-first toolbox for daily tasks such as PDF, image, data, text, and lightweight media utilities.
aboutPage.p2: Our default design goal is local processing when feasible, with static hosting for speed, reliability, and lower operational risk.
aboutPage.p3: Some tools call third-party APIs. Those tools display their own notices. Please avoid uploading confidential files to any online tool.
aboutPage.p4: Operator: PracticalKit is operated by an individual publisher (sole maintainer), not a registered company, unless otherwise stated here in the future.
aboutPage.p5: For legal, copyright, or abuse notices, use the Contact page. Response times may vary; please include enough detail to investigate.
aboutPage.p6: Content quality commitment: PracticalKit is not a link farm, doorway page, or mass-generated template site. Each tool exists to solve a concrete workflow (for example, comparing JSON safely in the browser, or converting units without sending your numbers to our servers). We pair those tools with human-written explanations of limits, failure modes, and privacy implications so a reader can understand value before interacting.
aboutPage.p7: Editorial process: new utilities ship with a short rationale in the Blog or changelog, not silently. When a tool depends on a third-party API, the page states the provider, what data crosses the network, and what you should not paste there. When processing is local-only, we say so explicitly and describe what still leaves your device (for example, downloads you choose to save).
aboutPage.p8: Safety and prohibited uses: do not use this site to process illegal material, to harass others, to bypass security controls, or to build spam or malware delivery pipelines. We may remove or restrict tools if abuse patterns appear. Technical limits (file size, codec support, memory) are documented on the relevant pages because overstated claims are also a form of low-quality publishing.
aboutPage.p9: Copyright and originality: tool descriptions, blog posts, and policy text on this domain are written for PracticalKit. Open-source libraries used in the browser stack are credited in repository metadata where applicable; we do not scrape third-party articles to pad word count.
aboutPage.p10: Corrections: if you believe a page contains a factual error about data handling or a tool capability, email the address on the Contact page with the URL and evidence. We will correct substantiated mistakes and note significant changes in the Blog when they affect safety or privacy.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.p1: We do not require account registration to use the core tools.
privacyPage.p2: For most tools, processing is completed in your browser and files are not sent to our servers by default.
privacyPage.p3: We may collect basic analytics, crash, and anti-abuse telemetry to keep the service stable and secure.
privacyPage.p4: Ads are provided by Google AdSense and may use cookies or similar technologies based on your region and consent settings.
privacyPage.p5: Third-party APIs are used only on pages that explicitly mention them. Review each provider's policy before submitting sensitive data.
privacyPage.p6: Analytics: we may use Google Analytics 4 (GA4) with IP anonymization enabled, only after you accept non-essential cookies via the banner (where shown). GA helps us understand which pages are used and how the site performs.
privacyPage.p7: Cookies: essential cookies may be needed for theme or UI preferences. Marketing/analytics cookies (such as GA) are optional and respect your choice in the cookie banner. You can change your mind by clearing site data for this website or using browser controls.
privacyPage.p8: Privacy-friendly analytics: when enabled for a deployment, we may load Plausible (or a compatible self-hosted script) to count page views in aggregate. It does not use third-party advertising cookies. This is off unless the site operator sets it in environment variables.

termsPage.title: Terms of Use
termsPage.p1: The service is provided as-is without warranty. Tool outputs should be reviewed before legal, medical, financial, or safety-critical use.
termsPage.p2: You must not use this site for illegal content, abuse, scraping attacks, malware distribution, or other harmful activity.
termsPage.p3: You are responsible for the files and content you process. Ensure you have permission to upload and transform that content.
termsPage.p4: We may update, suspend, or remove tools to keep the service compliant, secure, and sustainable.

disclaimerPage.title: Disclaimer
disclaimerPage.p1: This website provides general-purpose utility tools and does not provide professional legal, tax, financial, medical, or engineering advice.
disclaimerPage.p2: Compression ratios and conversion quality can vary by input characteristics, especially for noisy images or videos.
disclaimerPage.p3: No guarantee is made that every file format edge case is supported. Keep backups of your original files.

contactPage.title: Contact
contactPage.p1: For business, copyright, abuse, or policy requests, contact us at:
contactPage.p2: When reporting a bug, include browser version, locale, tool page, and reproducible steps.

cookieConsent.message: We use optional analytics cookies (Google Analytics) to improve the site. You can accept or decline.

blogPage.title: Blog & changelog
blogPage.lead: Long-form notes and changelog entries: engineering rationale, privacy boundaries, and what changed—written to stand alone as useful reading, not only as captions for tool UIs.

posts.title: Why utility sites get flagged for low-value content (and how we respond)
posts.body:
Automated quality systems look for thin pages: little original prose, heavy duplication, or a site that is mostly navigation and forms. A legitimate toolbox can be misclassified if crawlers mostly see short UI shells.

Our response is structural: keep the tools, but publish durable articles and policy pages that explain who operates the site, how each category of tool handles data, and what we will not promise. The About page now includes an explicit content-quality commitment and prohibited-use stance.

We also document third-party boundaries on every affected route, because undisclosed off-device processing is both a privacy issue and a trust issue for reviewers.

If you arrived from an advertising program notice, review those pages first, then sample a few tools: you should see consistent scope statements and predictable limits.

Finally, the changelog here is written for humans (what changed and why), not keyword stuffing. That balance—useful utilities plus readable editorial context—is the standard we maintain going forward.

posts.title: How we document local-only vs API-backed tools
posts.body:
PracticalKit defaults to client-side processing when the browser APIs are sufficient. That model reduces server liability and keeps many workflows private.

When an API is required (for example, live FX quotes or translation), the page must say so up front, before you paste data. We describe what is sent, what stays local, and what you should treat as sensitive.

We avoid blanket claims like 100% offline because media codecs, WASM paths, and browser bugs differ by platform. Instead we document typical limits and link to safer workflows when needed.

This documentation is part of the product, not a bolt-on footer, because unclear claims are another form of low-value content.

posts.title: Multilingual publishing: what we translate and what stays canonical
posts.body:
We ship English, Chinese, Russian, Spanish, and French coverage for core navigation and policies. Legal and safety-critical text aims for faithful meaning across locales, not machine filler.

Locale-specific pages share the same routes with a locale prefix so hreflang and canonical URLs stay coherent. When a translation lags a feature, we fall back to English for stability rather than publishing partial nonsense.

If you spot a misleading translation in a safety notice, contact us with the URL and locale; we prioritize corrections that affect consent, data handling, or risk statements.

posts.title: Why browser-first matters
posts.body:
When a tool runs in your browser, your files often never leave your device. That is not a guarantee for every page (some tools use third-party APIs when labeled), but it is the default design goal for PracticalKit.

When in doubt, read the notice on the tool page and avoid uploading sensitive data to any online service.

posts.title: Choosing PNG, WebP, or AVIF for screenshots
posts.body:
PNG is lossless and predictable. WebP and AVIF can be much smaller, but encoder support and visual differences vary by browser.

For archival or pixel-perfect needs, prefer PNG or a dedicated lossless workflow; for web delivery, try WebP/AVIF and compare visually.

posts.title: JSON diff: a practical workflow
posts.body:
When comparing configs or API payloads, pretty-print both sides and diff line-by-line. Stable key ordering helps reduce noisy differences.

Our JSON diff tool sorts object keys recursively so you can focus on real content changes.

posts.title: RGBV3D on the web: lossless only
posts.body:
The RGBV3D tool on this site is intended for lossless workflows. If you need maximum compatibility for sharing, export to PNG or WebP from the image tool after decoding.

Always keep originals when trying new formats.

posts.title: Site launch notes
posts.body:
PracticalKit went live as a static, multilingual toolbox with legal pages, a blog, and client-side utilities. We will keep iterating based on real usage and feedback.

Contact us if you find a bug or a policy concern.

pdf.title: PDF tools
pdf.split: Split into one PDF per page

image.title: Image tools
image.convertHint: Includes PNG, JPEG, WebP, AVIF (when supported), and RGBV3D (.rgbv3d) lossless export.

data.title: JSON / XML / YAML
data.privacyLine: Runs entirely in your browser — nothing is uploaded to our servers. Safe for configs and API samples.
data.shortcutHint: Tip: Ctrl+Enter (⌘+Enter on Mac) runs Format from the editor.
data.pathHint: Use dot/bracket path like user.profile.name or users[0].email
data.treeHint: Click a node to fill the path query. Expand rows to browse nested data.
data.treeNeedParse: Fix parse errors above to use the tree.
data.treeTooLarge: Structure is too large to render as a tree here. Use path query or shrink the payload.
data.schemaHint: Paste a JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12 / 2019-09 / draft-07). Validates the parsed data object (JSON, YAML, or XML after parsing).
data.schemaNeedParse: Fix parse errors above before validating against a schema.
data.convertNote: Parsed using the selected mode above (JSON / XML / YAML). Invalid input shows an error in the output.

calc.title: Calculator
calc.sciNote: Use sqrt, sin, cos, log, pi, e, …

translate.title: Translate (MyMemory free API)
translate.disclaimer: Third-party API — do not send secrets.

videoGif.title: Video convert
videoGif.note: Loads ffmpeg.wasm on demand — first run downloads ~25MB.

audio.title: Audio convert
audio.note: Experimental ffmpeg.wasm path; keep files small.

regex.title: Regex tester
regex.emptyPattern: Enter a pattern first (empty patterns are not run here).

rgbv3d.title: RGBV3D lossless
rgbv3d.body: Lossless RGB container (.rgbv3d) ships in the sibling folder rgbv3d-ecosystem. Browser viewer is bundled there; this page links to the format notes in PROJECT_OUTLINE.md when you self-host the viewer static files.
rgbv3d.encodeLead: Encode raster images to the vector-primary lossless profile in your browser.
rgbv3d.browseLead: Decode a .rgbv3d file and preview the image below — no server upload.
rgbv3d.encodeOnly: Encode and decode run locally in your browser.
rgbv3d.dirBitsHint: 8 vs 9 bits = 256 vs 512 direction bins on the sphere. Each 16×16 block still picks the smallest lossless mode (vector, Sub+deflate, plane stack, or raw). Previously, if classic Sub+deflate won for the whole image, 8/9 were ignored and outputs matched — this page fixes that by always using the block-adaptive path.
rgbv3d.downloadCli: Video containers and benchmarks: build rgbv3d-ecosystem/packages/cli (npm run build).

hashPage.title: SHA hash
hashPage.note: SHA-256 / 384 / 512 in your browser. Text or file.

jwtPage.title: JWT decoder
jwtPage.note: Decodes header and payload (Base64URL). Does not verify signatures.

csvJsonPage.title: CSV ↔ JSON
csvJsonPage.note: Simple CSV with quoted fields supported. For large files use desktop tools.

slugPage.title: Slug / URL segment
slugPage.note: Lowercase, diacritics stripped, spaces to hyphens.

cssMinPage.title: CSS minify
cssMinPage.note: Removes comments and extra whitespace. Check output before production.

jsonDiffPage.title: JSON diff

httpStatusPage.title: HTTP status
httpStatusPage.note: Find the meaning of an HTTP status code quickly.
httpStatusPage.hint_default: Verify the request URL, HTTP method, headers, body, and any proxy or server logs.
httpStatusPage.hint_400: Check JSON/XML shape, query parameters, and required fields; ensure Content-Type matches the payload.
httpStatusPage.hint_401: Confirm Authorization, cookies, or API keys; check token expiry and client/server clock skew.
httpStatusPage.hint_403: You may be signed in but not allowed; review roles, IP allowlists, and WAF or tenant rules.
httpStatusPage.hint_404: Confirm path and HTTP method; check routing, rewrites, soft-404s, and whether the resource was removed.
httpStatusPage.hint_405: Use a method allowed for this route (see Allow header or API docs).
httpStatusPage.hint_408: Increase client/server timeouts or reduce payload; look for slow upstreams or blocked sockets.
httpStatusPage.hint_409: Resolve conflicting state such as duplicate IDs, version conflicts, or concurrent edits.
httpStatusPage.hint_413: Compress or split uploads; raise body size limits only when appropriate.
httpStatusPage.hint_415: Use a Content-Type the server accepts (e.g. application/json vs multipart/form-data).
httpStatusPage.hint_422: Fix validation errors returned in the body; read field-level messages from the response.
httpStatusPage.hint_429: Slow down or backoff; honor Retry-After; check quotas and per-IP or per-key limits.
httpStatusPage.hint_500: Server-side fault; reproduce with logs; try a minimal request to isolate the handler.
httpStatusPage.hint_502: Upstream unreachable or bad TLS; verify load balancer targets and backend health.
httpStatusPage.hint_503: Overload or maintenance; retry with backoff; check health checks and circuit breakers.
httpStatusPage.hint_504: Gateway timed out waiting for upstream; extend timeouts or fix slow downstream services.
httpStatusPage.privacyLine: Reference table — same common codes browsers and APIs use. Click a code or filter the list.

apiSnippetPage.title: API snippets
apiSnippetPage.note: Generate request snippets for quick testing.

baseConvertPage.title: Base converter (BigInt)
baseConvertPage.note: Convert big integers across base2/base8/base10/base16/base36 without precision loss.
baseConvertPage.invalid: Invalid number for the selected base.
